chapter_title.en: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca

text: 3012 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا زَارَ اَلْحَاجُّ مِنْ مِنًى فَخَرَجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَجَازَ بُيُوتَ مَكَّةَ فَنَامَ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ مِنًى فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3012 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Al-Hakam, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a pilgrim visits from Mina, leaves Mecca, passes by the houses of Mecca, sleeps, and then wakes up before returning to Mina, there is no obligation upon him."
